untranslatable_reason: Pattern requires visual detection of two upward-sloping trend lines with specific slope differential and wedge divergence over time. Available indicators cannot mathematically construct or validate dual trend line slopes, their relative steepness comparison, or the wedge-spreading geometry. Entry conditions 1-4 require pattern recognition beyond the scope of technical indicators (trend line drawing, slope measurement, geometric validation). Only the price breakout (condition 5: 'price pierces bottom trend line') has been partially approximated as close crossing below previous low.
translation_notes: This is a classical chart pattern requiring visual/geometric analysis. The breakout condition (price below bottom trend line) has been translated as close < prev_low as a simple approximation. Exit condition uses mean reversion to sma_20 as a reasonable counter-trend exit since AUTHOR_SILENT was provided. The 75% success rate in bear markets and downward breakout direction informed the SHORT direction choice. However, the core pattern definition cannot be properly encoded without trend line detection capabilities.
